name: default-deny
description: No satisfied permit produces Cedar's default deny.
policies:
@id("never")
permit(principal, action == Kontext::Action::"ToolUse", resource) when { false };

name: bash-command-allow
description: A runtime-native command field can authorize an exact tool name.
policies:
@id("bash_status")
permit(principal, action == Kontext::Action::"ToolUse", resource == Kontext::Tool::"Bash") when { context has command && context.command like "git status*" };

name: exact-external-tool-name
description: An external runtime tool name is preserved without provider canonicalization.
policies:
@id("create_pr")
permit(principal, action == Kontext::Action::"ToolUse", resource == Kontext::Tool::"mcp__github__create_pull_request") when { context has owner && context.owner == "kontext-security" };

name: principal-is-structured-and-constrained
description: A policy constrained to Alice does not match Bob's structured principal binding.

name: forbid-overrides-permit
description: A satisfied forbid overrides a broad permit.
policies:
@id("deny_rm")
forbid(principal, action == Kontext::Action::"ToolUse", resource == Kontext::Tool::"Bash") when { !(context has command) || context.command like "rm *" };

name: ask-only-allow
description: An allow determined entirely by prompt permits derives ask.

name: normal-permit-defeats-ask
description: A normal determining permit independently authorizes a request that also matches an ask permit.
policies:
@id("allow_status")
permit(principal, action == Kontext::Action::"ToolUse", resource == Kontext::Tool::"Bash") when { context has command && context.command like "git status*" };

name: open-nested-context
description: Nested records, sets, decimals, and omitted nulls remain available without a closed context schema.
policies:
@id("structured_input")
permit(principal, action == Kontext::Action::"ToolUse", resource == Kontext::Tool::"CustomTool") when { context has risk && context.risk == decimal("1.25") && context has options && context.options has force && context.options.force && context has tags && context.tags.contains("prod") };

name: missing-context-key
description: Guarded access to an absent context key is non-applicable and falls through to deny.
policies:
@id("requires_command")
permit(principal, action == Kontext::Action::"ToolUse", resource) when { context has command && context.command like "git status*" };
